title: SkillNet: Create, Evaluate, and Connect AI Skills
description: Infrastructure for systematically accumulating and transferring AI skills at scale, featuring a repository of over 200,000 skills and demonstrating significant performance improvements across multiple agent benchmarks.

title: Capability Thresholds and Manufacturing Topology: How Embodied Intelligence Triggers Phase Transitions in Economic Geography
description: Theoretical framework examining how embodied AI capabilities reshape manufacturing geography and supply chains through phase transitions in site selection and production organization.

title: Progressive Refinement Regulation for Accelerating Diffusion Language Model Decoding
description: Framework controlling token-level refinement in diffusion models through progressive training, achieving substantial acceleration while maintaining generation quality.

title: Discovering mathematical concepts through a multi-agent system
description: Multi-agent computational discovery system that autonomously poses conjectures and attempts proofs, successfully recovering mathematical concepts like homology from polyhedral data.

title: Adaptive Memory Admission Control for LLM Agents
description: Framework treating memory admission as a structured decision problem with five interpretable factors, achieving superior precision-recall tradeoffs for long-term agent memory.

title: Self-Attribution Bias: When AI Monitors Go Easy on Themselves
description: Study demonstrating how language models evaluate actions more favorably when framed as their own versus user-presented, revealing monitoring vulnerabilities in agentic systems.

title: ECG-MoE: Mixture-of-Expert Electrocardiogram Foundation Model
description: Hybrid architecture integrating temporal features with cardiac period-aware expert modules for ECG analysis, achieving state-of-the-art performance with 40% faster inference.

title: Towards automated data analysis: A guided framework for LLM-based risk estimation
description: Framework integrating generative AI with human supervision for dataset risk assessment, enabling LLMs to identify properties, propose clustering, and interpret results.

title: When Agents Persuade: Propaganda Generation and Mitigation in LLMs
description: Study analyzing LLM tendency to generate propagandistic content and exploring mitigation through fine-tuning approaches, finding ORPO most effective.

title: Using Vision + Language Models to Predict Item Difficulty
description: Multimodal approach leveraging vision and language models for psychometric analysis, achieving lowest error through combined visual and textual features.

title: Decorrelating the Future: Joint Frequency Domain Learning for Spatio-temporal Forecasting
description: Proposes FreST Loss, a frequency-enhanced training objective that extends supervision to the joint spatio-temporal spectrum using Joint Fourier Transform to better capture dependencies across space and time in graph-structured signals.

title: Machine Learning for Complex Systems Dynamics: Detecting Bifurcations in Dynamical Systems with Deep Neural Networks
description: Introduces equilibrium-informed neural networks (EINNs) to identify critical thresholds and catastrophic regime shifts in complex dynamical systems by analyzing learned parameter landscapes.

title: FedEMA-Distill: Exponential Moving Average Guided Knowledge Distillation for Robust Federated Learning
description: Combines exponential moving average of global models with ensemble knowledge distillation from client logits to improve federated learning under heterogeneous data and adversarial conditions.

title: Delta-Crosscoder: Robust Crosscoder Model Diffing in Narrow Fine-Tuning Regimes
description: Presents a method combining BatchTopK sparsity with delta-based loss to identify latent directions responsible for fine-tuned behaviors in narrow fine-tuning scenarios across multiple model sizes.

title: Thin Keys, Full Values: Reducing KV Cache via Low-Dimensional Attention Selection
description: Demonstrates that keys require lower dimensionality than values in transformer attention, achieving 75% key cache reduction with minimal quality loss through asymmetric attention design.

title: Agent Memory Below the Prompt: Persistent Q4 KV Cache for Multi-Agent LLM Inference on Edge Devices
description: Addresses multi-agent LLM memory constraints on edge devices by persisting quantized KV caches to disk, enabling up to 136x speedup in cache restoration for concurrent agent inference.

title: Flowers: A Warp Drive for Neural PDE Solvers
description: Introduces Flowers, a neural architecture using multihead warps for learning PDE solution operators, achieving superior performance on 2D and 3D time-dependent benchmarks without Fourier multipliers or attention.

title: Uncertainty-Calibrated Spatiotemporal Field Diffusion with Sparse Supervision
description: Presents SOLID, a mask-conditioned diffusion framework trained end-to-end with sparse sensor observations only, achieving calibrated uncertainty estimates for physical field reconstruction.

title: ZorBA: Zeroth-order Federated Fine-tuning of LLMs with Heterogeneous Block Activation
description: Proposes zeroth-order optimization framework with heterogeneous block activation for federated LLM fine-tuning, reducing VRAM usage by up to 62% while maintaining convergence performance.

title: ASFL: An Adaptive Model Splitting and Resource Allocation Framework for Split Federated Learning
description: Introduces adaptive model splitting framework over wireless networks that reduces total delay and energy consumption by up to 75% and 80% respectively through joint optimization.

title: CTRL-RAG: Contrastive Likelihood Reward Based Reinforcement Learning for Context-Faithful RAG Models
description: Proposes a novel reward framework for RAG training that optimizes log-likelihood gaps between responses with and without evidence, improving faithfulness and context sensitivity in language models.

title: Semantic Containment as a Fundamental Property of Emergent Misalignment
description: Investigates how semantic triggers in harmful fine-tuning create compartmentalized model behavior without benign data contrast, revealing safety vulnerabilities in language models.

title: Probing Memes in LLMs: A Paradigm for the Entangled Evaluation World
description: Introduces the Probing Memes paradigm reconceptualizing LLM evaluation through model-item interactions, revealing hidden capability structures across diverse datasets and models.

title: Unpacking Human Preference for LLMs: Demographically Aware Evaluation with the HUMAINE Framework
description: Presents HUMAINE framework for multidimensional human preference evaluation across demographic groups, revealing significant preference heterogeneity based on user age and model characteristics.

title: SalamahBench: Toward Standardized Safety Evaluation for Arabic Language Models
description: Introduces comprehensive safety benchmark with 8,170 prompts for evaluating Arabic language models, identifying substantial safety alignment variations across state-of-the-art models.

title: One Size Does Not Fit All: Token-Wise Adaptive Compression for KV Cache
description: Proposes DynaKV for dynamic KV cache compression allocating rates per token, achieving aggressive compression ratios while maintaining generation quality in language models.

title: Additive Multi-Step Markov Chains and the Curse of Dimensionality in Large Language Models
description: Explores N-order additive Markov chains as theoretical approximations for LLM dynamics, establishing correspondences with step-wise memory functions and information temperature.

title: Simulating Meaning, Nevermore! Introducing ICR: A Semiotic-Hermeneutic Metric for Evaluating Meaning in LLM Text Summaries
description: Proposes ICR metric integrating semiotics and hermeneutics for evaluating semantic accuracy in LLM-generated summaries, revealing performance gaps in contextually grounded meaning.

title: Multiclass Hate Speech Detection with RoBERTa-OTA: Integrating Transformer Attention and Graph Convolutional Networks
description: Combines RoBERTa embeddings with ontology-guided attention and graph neural networks for hate speech detection, achieving 96.04% accuracy with minimal parameter overhead.

title: The Thinking Boundary: Quantifying Reasoning Suitability of Multimodal Tasks via Dual Tuning
description: Proposes Dual Tuning framework to assess when reasoning benefits multimodal tasks, establishing boundaries for reasoning suitability across diverse domains and challenging the reasoning-for-all paradigm.

title: Partnering with Mozilla to improve Firefox's security
description: Anthropic collaboration to enhance Firefox security capabilities

title: Where things stand with the Department of War
description: Statement from Dario Amodei regarding discussions and position

title: Statement on the comments from Secretary of War Pete Hegseth
description: Company response to Secretary of War and guidance for customers

title: Statement from Dario Amodei on our discussions with the Department of War
description: CEO statement addressing national security applications of AI

title: Anthropic acquires Vercept to advance Claude's computer use capabilities
description: Acquisition announcement to enhance Claude's technical abilities

title: Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy: Version 3.0
description: Updated policy framework for responsible AI development

title: Detecting and preventing distillation attacks
description: Research on security measures against model distillation

title: Making frontier cybersecurity capabilities available to defenders
description: Claude Code Security scans codebases for vulnerabilities and suggests patches

title: Anthropic and the Government of Rwanda sign MOU for AI in health and education
description: Memorandum of understanding for AI applications in development sectors

title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 4.6
description: Sonnet 4.6 delivers frontier performance across coding, agents, and work

title: How DINO and SAM are Helping Modernize Essential Medical Triage Practices
description: Teams at the University of Pennsylvania leverage advanced AI models to automate emergency response procedures.

title: Reducing Government Costs and Increasing Access to Greenspaces in the United Kingdom with DINO
description: Meta's DINOv2 model enhances reforestation efforts globally, helping UK government reduce costs and expand greenspace access.

title: How Orakl Oncology is using DINOv2 to accelerate cancer treatment discovery
description: Orakl Oncology combines experimental lab insights with machine learning to advance cancer research and drug development.

title: The Universities Space Research Association Applies Segment Anything Model for Responding to Flood Emergencies
description: Partnership supporting U.S. Geological Survey water observation systems using Meta's AI models for disaster response.

title: Introducing SAM Audio: The First Unified Multimodal Model for Audio Separation
description: SAM Audio isolates sounds from complex audio mixtures through natural multimodal prompts like text and visual cues.

title: Introducing SAM 3D: Powerful 3D Reconstruction for Physical World Images
description: Two state-of-the-art models enable object/scene reconstruction and human body shape estimation from physical world imagery.

title: How Conservation X Labs Is Using Segment Anything Model 3 for Endangered Wildlife Monitoring
description: Start-ups apply Meta's Segment Anything Models to strengthen on-the-ground conservation expertise.

title: ExecuTorch Adoption in Reality Labs: Powering On-Device AI Across Meta Devices
description: ExecuTorch, Meta's lightweight inference engine, enables on-device AI capabilities across Meta's app ecosystem.

title: Omnilingual ASR: Advancing Automatic Speech Recognition for 1,600+ Languages
description: Meta introduces a suite of models providing automatic speech recognition for over 1,600 languages worldwide.

title: Introducing Meta Segment Anything Model 3 and Segment Anything Playground
description: Explore the latest SAM releases and an interactive playground showcasing advanced AI capabilities.

title: Anthropic to challenge DOD's supply-chain label in court
description: Anthropic's CEO plans legal action against the Department of Defense's designation of the company as a supply-chain risk, asserting most customers remain unaffected.
